key_insight: Density-based topology optimization can tailor plasmonic metasurfaces to maximize attractive optical trapping forces on nanoparticles.
engineering_relevance: The approach enables selective, potentially manufacturable nanoparticle trapping for biosensing, microfabrication, and quantum-system assembly.
method: Use Maxwell stress tensor to compute optical forces on nanoparticles.
Optimize metasurface topology under normal-incidence monochromatic illumination.
Compare free-form designs and manufacturable planar designs with imposed constraints.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimized designs for different nanoparticle sizes and materials, showing higher trapping stiffness for smaller nanoparticles.
watch_item: Performance depends strongly on particle size and material, so designs may need retuning for each target application and fabrication constraint.
paper_title: Topology optimized plasmonic metasurfaces for optical trapping of nanoparticles
